chunks.text: One morning, the alarm rang
but I was not afraid to get up — I was simply bored.
I had been annoyed by the same routine repeating itself every day.
I tried to make coffee, but the beans had run out.
I walked to the neighborhood cafe and ordered coffee from a clerk I knew by face.
I had no cash, so I charged it to my card.
On the way back, I met the elderly lady next door.
" Yesterday I buried my precious ring in the garden," she said.
" An insect bit my hand, and when I waved it, the ring flew off.
I did not feel it a bother.
I was glad to help.
After digging for fifteen minutes with a shovel, a golden ring appeared from the soil.
She laughed and said, " What a charm you have, young man.
Back at home, a friend called.
" Will you agree to come camping next week?
" he asked.
I accepted at once.
I would need to borrow a tent.
A day I had thought would be boring turned out, by the end
to be a good day blessed by circumstance.
In the ordinary flow of daily life, small events and conversation are always waiting.
Perhaps every day is, after all, a little more comfortable than we think.
